mnemonics: P3: Product is the final Prize, Process is the Path to get it. Both require Quality Management.
keyPoints: Product Quality Management focuses on the attributes of the final deliverable (e.g., functionality, reliability, usability).
Process Quality Management focuses on the methods and activities used to develop the product (e.g., standards, reviews, methodologies).
A high-quality process is designed to consistently produce a high-quality product and is often measured through metrics like defect density or cycle time.
commonMistakes: Focusing solely on the final product and neglecting the process, which leads to unpredictable quality and repeated errors.
Assuming a high-quality process automatically guarantees a high-quality product, without validating the end result against user needs.
